title: Sorindeia suaveolens
intro: Sorindeia suaveolens is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Anacardiaceae and is also known as the 'Sweet Sorindeia'. The plant has been traditionally used in African folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Sorindeia suaveolens include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions in traditional medicine.
